## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->

Intro and tutorial videos flicker black, and also some tutorial videos display a test pattern.

## Reproduction

- While in game hit the "i" key to bring up the skills menu, however over the skills to see the tutorial videos

Video replicating problem:
[plaguetale.webm](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/43387963/201447994-868957f0-3cfd-444c-8f9f-6a78e60731d0.webm)

[steam-1182900-experimental-bleeding-edge-7.0.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/9993761/steam-1182900-experimental-bleeding-edge-7.0.log)

## System Information
- GPU: AMD RX 5700
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 23.0.0-rc4
- Kernel version: 6.2.0-0.rc6.20230202git9f266ccaa2f5.46.fc38.x86_64
- Proton version: experimental-bleeding-edge-7.0-34887-20230204

## Symptoms

Re-tested the game and the intro and tutorial videos now play back properly, without the flickering or test patterns.
The only remaining minor problem I see is that the skill tutorial videos do not loop as they probably should. 

## Reproduction
- While in game hit the "i" key to bring up the skills menu, hover the mouse pointer over a skill. And watch the tutorial video to the end.

[steam-1182900.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10609011/steam-1182900.log)
[system_info.txt](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10609012/system_info.txt)

In the logs I did spot "fixme:mfplat:media_engine_SetLoop", I guess that is related to the tutorial video looping not working.

## System Information
- GPU: AMD RX 5700 XT
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 22.3.6, LLVM 15.0.7 
- Kernel version: 6.2.5-arch1-1 x86_64
- Proton version: experimental-7.0-20230313

## Symptoms

Bottleneck while running the game, it seems that the game just can't go below 30ms frame-time. This causes the game to run at max ~35 fps no matter what settings/resolution I set up. Decreasing graphics fidelity causes GPU usage to drop, but fps/frame-time stay the same.

I also get only 20 fps in the main menu.

## Reproduction

Run the game.

## Attempted fixes

Tried using stable proton (black screen) and proton GE (same issue). Tried a variety of launch options as recommended at protonDB, however nothing worker.

The game is installed on a NVME drive, there is enough GPU VRAM/system RAM available and the CPU isn't the bottleneck.

## Logs

[steam-1182900.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10972334/steam-1182900.log)

Some suspicious things I noticed in the logs:
- `warn:  OpenXR: Unable to get required Vulkan instance extensions size`
- `fixme:thread:SetThreadIdealProcessor (000000000000013C 10): stub`
- `warn:vkd3d-proton:D3D12GetDebugInterface: Returning DXGI_ERROR_SDK_COMPONENT_MISSING.`
- `warn:vkd3d-proton:rs_desc_from_d3d12: Ignoring MultisampleEnable 0x1.`
- `fixme:vkd3d-proton:vkd3d_create_texture_uav_view: Unhandled depth view`
- `warn:vkd3d-proton:vkd3d_allocate_device_memory: Memory allocation failed, falling back to system memory.`

## Footage

![fhd@low](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/113016311/225099015-3f0c25f9-188c-45c5-a952-2d5f77b35ba2.png)

![signal-2023-03-14-102422_002](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/113016311/225099719-86e9a47b-ff69-44bd-bf1f-e5f1ba477876.jpeg)

@isitcrazythough I can see from your screenshot that you are using amdvlk as a vulkan driver and not mesa. Amdvlk has terrible performance with all d3d12 games, so this is the issue here. You should install `vulkan-radeon` and `lib32-vulkan-radeon`, and uninstall `amdvlk` and `lib32-amdvlk`.
Oh wow, you are right. Thanks for that, saved me there. :+1:

This game (among others like Armored Core VI and The Talos Principle 2) are crashing to desktop on my rig randomly. I've tried several things like changing lots of Proton versions, disabling and enabling SAM or even disabling MangoHUD. None work.

Everytime it crashes, it registers a new seh:dispatch_exception EXCEPTION_ACCESS_VIOLATION exception (code=c0000005) raised in the logs.

Log: [steam-1182900.log](https://github.com/user-attachments/files/16480805/steam-1182900.log)
My computer: https://gist.github.com/MekaDragon/ae7e3702f9e395b265577dac5bd782cf

I don't know what else to try, but this is really annoying, and it's preventing me to even enjoy games.

That Proton log suggests a stack overflow inside RADV, which points to a driver bug. I tried reproducing this, but couldn't, and all generated SPIR-V looks valid.

Is DXR enabled? It worked fine for me, but it's not impossible it explains a few things. I would try:

- VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr
- Try updating Mesa to something more recent
- Make sure you haven't configured a small stack ulimit or something like that

I appreciate the response! So I just tested launching it with VKD3D_CONFIG=nodxr and the same thing happened. My stack ulimit appears to be 8192, I haven't messed with that so I don't think it's overly small. As far as I can tell my Mesa drivers are as up to date as my OS is shipping. 

If you have any other ideas I could try I'll give it a go. If not that's okay, I appreciate the effort. I'll keep an eye on driver updates and try it again in the future, see if it sorts itself out.

@maschere 

Thanks for the info, this is most helpful.

Indeed, the problem is in TCP non-blocking socket transfer between main process and a python process. The socket on the main process is not explicitly set as non-blocking but is initialized as such after accept() because the listening socket is non-blocking. What is specific about sending (probably) that image is that send() is called with larger length than for the other objects (~250k). On Proton / Linux with the set up SO_SNDBUF it currently always ends up in short write (i. e., the actually written by send() and returned length is smaller than requested; while tweaking SO_SNDBUF would not be a solution here, it may still end up with short write if there is some congestion on delivering the data). The game actually tries to handle short write and calls send() again advancing send buffer pointer to the number of bytes returned by previous (short) send(). The problem is that it doesn't decrement the amount of bytes to send (e. g, while advancing buffer start, calls next sends with exact same length as the initial one). That results in send() attempting to read past the allocated buffer and failing with WSAEFAULT (that doesn't cause access violation because the data access happens on the kernel side and results in the error without sending anything). The loop which does this repeated sending and has this issue starts at address 0x141d8e400 in the main executable (for the default game branch as of now). But exactly the same behaviour is also observed in the python process, I didn't look where exactly that happens there.

Why that works on Windows is because Windows actually never does short send() (regardless of what is said in [1]). It may return -1 and set WSAGetLastError() to WSAEWOULDBLOCK (i. e., send nothing) or queue the full requested length for sending (of virtually unlimited size, I've tested that up to 2GB) and return the requested length. So we actually have a compatibility issue in Proton. I sent a patch Wine upstream [2] for that, but this is a bit tricky and has potential of breaking a lot if I missed something, so I am not inclined to rush that into Proton and prefer to wait for some review comments first.

Meanwhile, maybe that can be fixed in game? That would probably be more robust regardless (by properly reducing the length on those repeated sends)? I don't know though if that is inside the game code itself or inside some library used, if the latter then probably would be ideal to let the library developers know.

1. https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/win32/api/winsock2/nf-winsock2-send , "Return value" section: "If no error occurs, send returns the total number of bytes sent, which can be less than the number requested to be sent in the len parameter."
2. https://gitlab.winehq.org/wine/wine/-/merge_requests/6490
@maschere That short send() issue should be hopefully fixed in the current Proton Experimental ([bleeding-edge] branch).
@gofman thanks, that is great news. Is this branch already available on SteamDeck? I just tried it with "Proton Experimental" there, but it was the same behaviour as before.
Hello @maschere, you need to switch beta branches on Proton Experimental to access the bleeding edge automated build. https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/wiki/Proton-Versions#proton-bleeding-edge
Thank you, I can confirm it's now working as expected and identical to the windows version.

The game is Steam Deck verified, it should work without issues.
Having the same problem on the Steam Deck. Have tried factory reset the steam deck and change the protonDB version, but seems to be a problem with the game

On Steam Deck, I believe the game was running well on Proton 7.0-4, but now, it crashes a short time after reaching the title screen on Proton 7.0-5 (and Proton 7.0-6rc, if I successfully activated it). A crash report window appears afterwards, but it disappears immediately.
For me downloading Proton GE fixed the issue and I have experienced no more crashes. So I guess it was a problem in the latest Proton versions.

Hello @a-will, please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options, reproduce the regression, and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.) Also, please copy your system information from Steam (`Steam` -> `Help` -> `System Information`) and put it in a [gist](https://gist.github.com/), then include a link to the gist in this issue report.

The current evidence hints that there was a change in game behavior with the game's Ver1.0.1 update on 2022-11-11 instead of there being a regression in Proton.
Proton GE 42 fixed this for me
Proton ge is not officially supported by Valve

@kisak-valve: Here's the [system info gist](https://gist.github.com/a-will/ef367ed93b4c65f4d5a9a171a14defa9).

And [steam-1850510.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/10261129/steam-1850510.log)

My memory may not be the most reliable thing here, but for what it's worth, I believe I had been playing Ver1.0.1 without issues prior to some non-game update on the Steam Deck (one where the client or system rebooted). I may be incorrectly assuming that included a Proton update from 7.0-4 to 7.0-5 and attributing the crash to a version number that didn't match my Ubuntu desktop at the time.

Now the two do match, and the game runs fine on my Ubuntu desktop with Proton 7.0-5. So, this crash may be specific to the Deck somehow.

@a-will Could you try renaming/moving the prefix on the deck where the game is currently failing with Proton 7.0-5?  I am asking for you to do this instead of deleting the prefix, because if this does "fix" the game, it would be really helpful to have a copy of the broken prefix :). You can do this relatively easily by going into desktop mode and opening a terminal, and then running
"mv ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/1850510 ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/1850510.bak".  Then reboot to get back to SteamOS deck mode, and try relaunching the game with Proton 7.0-5.

@alasky17 I'll note that this game currently resides on a microSD card. There was a prefix for this game both in the home folder's library and in the microSD card's library. I tried renaming them individually, then together, but the crash still occurred.

One interesting observation is that the prefix did not get recreated on the microSD card, and the last mtime for files there looks like it may be just before the crashing started happening. I can't say whether that's a red herring, but perhaps I ought to try moving the game to the home folder library and see if it makes a difference.

@a-will That is very interesting indeed.  By default, the prefix for games stored on an SD card should be stored in the home folder's library on the deck (this is not true on desktop, only on the deck).  It does hint that something is going wrong if you have a prefix there - and also give me something to try here too :D

@a-will I think that we figured out the mystery.  The game had not been properly flagged as requiring h264.  It likely worked on your desktop because you had already played some game there which unlocked h264.  The game *should* be properly flagged now.  It might require a restart to pick up the flag, but I'm hoping that now the game will suddenly work :)
@alasky17 Awesome sleuthing! I can confirm the crash no longer occurs. Thanks for the fix!

Hello @atrauzzi, please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options, reproduce the crash, and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.)

@atrauzzi Just as a note - we can't guarantee we can get the game to work for you even if you repurchase.
I know.  Still, we gotta try.
